statement.headerDoc:
STEP 957 — Arithmetic Geometry Open Problems
  藤本伸樹 2026-04-21

  Five open problems in arithmetic geometry and number theory:

  A. TATE CONJECTURE (Tate 1963)
     For a smooth projective variety X/k (k finitely generated):
     cycle class map CH^r(X) ⊗ ℚ_ℓ → H^{2r}_{et}(X̄, ℚ_ℓ(r))^{Gal(k̄/k)}
     is surjective. STATUS: OPEN for general X.
     Proved: abelian varieties over finite fields (Tate 1966), surfaces (Faltings 1983 for abelian).

  B. MORDELL CONJECTURE → FALTINGS' THEOREM (1983)
     Curves of genus ≥ 2 over number fields have finitely many rational points.
     STATUS: THEOREM
